suffix: The ranges of the American and Chinese alligator extend into regions that sometimes experience periods of frost in winter . Being ectothermic , the internal body temperature of crocodilians falls as the temperature drops , and they become sluggish . They may become more active on warm days , but do not usually feed at all during the winter . In cold weather , they remain submerged with their tails in deeper , less cold water and their nostrils just projecting through the surface . If ice forms on the water , they maintain ice @-@ free breathing holes , and there have been occasions when their snouts have become frozen into the ice .

Despite the maternal care they receive , hatchlings commonly fall to predation . While the female is transporting some to the nursery area , others are picked off by predators that lurk near the nest . The hatchlings are a source of food for most of the creatures that feed on eggs , and are also subject to aquatic attacks by turtles , fish , and snakes . Birds of prey take their toll , and in any clutch there may be malformed individuals that are unlikely to survive . In northern Australia , the survival rate for saltwater crocodile hatchlings is only twenty @-@ five percent , but with each succeeding year of life this improves , reaching sixty percent by year five . Mortality rates are fairly low among subadult and adults of small species , though they are occasionally preyed on by large cats and snakes . The jaguar and the giant otter may prey on caimans in South America .

Is Dropbox better at managing memory than Google Drive? - marcamillion
http://i.imgur.com/C7hnD.png

======
marcamillion
Could Dropbox really have better engineers than the Google Drive team?

Or could this be one of those examples of specialization - i.e. because the
entire company at Dropbox is focused on just this one problem - so they are
better at it, whereas the Google Drive team is just a small team within
Google.

As a result Dropbox has more resources at their disposal to make a better
product?

If that is the case, this could be the quintessential case study on why
startups shouldn't fear large incumbents. Not only are large companies
bureacratic, but more often than not they will have institutionalized inertia
that prevents them from dedicating the resources to take you on - until it's
too late.

Once upon a time, there were two friends named Bob and Jane. Every day they would play in the park together, having lots of fun. One day, they decided to build something special, so they collected some logs and started to build a chair.

Bob found a nice and small log, just perfect for the back of the chair. When they finished building, they both decided to take a break. Jane looked around the park and suggested that they should have a picnic.

Bob nodded and started to go home to get what they needed for the picnic, but before leaving he said "Let's decide something. Whoever can do five somersaults in a row gets the small chair."

When Bob returned with the food and drinks. Jane had already got in position and she was getting ready to do the somersault. With determination, she did five somersaults in a row, and Bob had to admit defeated.

When looking for reliable online sources, it is important to keep a few things in mind:
- Check the author or creator of the source, as well as their credentials.
- Make sure the source is up-to-date, as information can quickly become outdated.
- Read multiple sources and compare the information.
- Look for sources from trusted organizations, such as government websites, educational institutions, and reliable media outlets.
- Check for evidence to back up the claims made in the source.

One day, a little girl went for a walk. She had been walking for a while when she heard a beautiful sound. It was coming from deep in the woods.

The little girl walked through the sunny woods until she found a clearing. In the clearing, she found an orange violin. She had never seen a violin that colour before.

The little girl was very excited. She took hold of the violin and started to play. The sweet sound of the strings was magical.

The little girl kept the violin with her and brought it home. She loved to play it and would practice every day.
